---
title: "Spatial QC report generation"
description: "The same QC report as single-cell, with a spatial ingestion method."
categories: [openpipeline_qc]
order: 25
---

[`openpipeline_qc`]{.pkg-tag .pkg-openpipeline_qc}<span class="swatch openpipeline_qc"></span>

Spatial data uses the **same** `generate_qc_report` workflow as single-cell data,
from the `openpipeline_qc` package. The only difference is `--ingestion_method`,
which tells the report how the data was ingested so it computes the right metrics
for the platform.

## Options

| Workflow | Use when |
|---|---|
| [`generate_qc_report`](../reference/openpipeline_qc/v0.3.0/workflows/generate_qc_report.qmd) | Spatial `.h5mu`; set `--ingestion_method` to the platform |

## Example: Xenium QC report

For example, for a [spatially-ingested](ingest-spatial.qmd) Xenium object, set
`--ingestion_method xenium` (or `visium` / `cosmx`):

```bash
nextflow run https://packages.viash-hub.com/vsh/openpipeline_qc.git \
  -r v0.3.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/generate_qc_report/main.nf \
  --id cohort_01 \
  --input cohort_01.h5mu \
  --ingestion_method xenium \
  --output qc_report.html \
  --publish_dir results/
```

## Key choices

- **`--ingestion_method`** must match your platform: `xenium`, `visium`, or
  `cosmx`.
- For a whole cohort, pass a [`param_list`](process-many-samples.qmd) with one
  entry per sample and `ingestion_method` set once as a shared argument.
- Everything else, metrics, options, output, is identical to the core
  [QC report](qc-report.qmd).

## Output

A self-contained `qc_report.html`. Use it to choose thresholds for
[spatial sample processing](process-spatial.qmd).
